articles.title: Buenos Aires Court Orders Polymarket Blocked in Argentina
articles.summary: A Buenos Aires court has ordered internet service providers to block access to Polymarket, effectively banning the prediction betting platform in Argentina. The move signals growing regulatory hostility toward crypto-based prediction markets in Latin America, potentially forcing operators to choose between compliance costs and abandoning entire regions.

articles.title: Prediction markets more like gambling than investing, Americans say
articles.summary: Americans view prediction markets as closer to gambling than investing, according to new survey data. This perception problem will keep limiting mainstream adoption and regulatory acceptance, no matter how accurate the markets prove to be.

articles.title: Prediction market Kalshi takes legal stand against Arizona’s gambling laws
articles.summary: Kalshi is challenging Arizona's gambling laws in court, presumably to defend its prediction market operations in the state. If successful, the case could set important legal precedent for how prediction markets are regulated nationwide, potentially opening doors in other restrictive jurisdictions.

articles.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Got the Oscars About 80 Percent Right
articles.summary: Prediction markets Polymarket and Kalshi correctly predicted roughly 80% of Oscar winners this year, showing their growing accuracy in entertainment forecasting. The strong performance validates that betting markets can cut through Hollywood hype and insider politics to identify actual frontrunners, making them valuable tools for future awards season gambling.

articles.title: Make the Predictions Come True
articles.summary: Some prediction markets are shifting from pure forecasting to actively trying to influence the outcomes they're betting on. This blurs the line between predicting and manipulating events, which could either supercharge market accuracy or completely undermine their credibility as information sources.

articles.title: Perfection is nearly impossible: Kalshi is giving away $1 billion for a perfect bracket
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $1 billion to anyone who can correctly predict every game in March Madness, joining the long tradition of impossible bracket challenges that generate massive publicity while costing almost nothing. The stunt shows how prediction markets are using lottery-style promotions to attract mainstream attention, even though the mathematical odds make Warren Buffett's old $1 billion bracket bet look generous.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket correctly predicted 19 out of 24 Oscar winners. Here are the ones they missed.
articles.summary: Prediction markets Kalshi and Polymarket nailed 79% of Oscar winners this year, missing only 5 categories out of 24. The strong performance adds more credibility to betting markets as entertainment forecasting tools, especially as Hollywood awards become increasingly predictable through industry sentiment and campaign spending.

articles.title: The Polymarket Gamblers Betting on the Value of Your Apartment
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are now placing bets on real estate prices, turning your local housing market into another prediction market playground. This signals prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into everyday economic indicators that actually affect people's wallets, creating new opportunities for informed bettors to profit from housing knowledge.

articles.title: Wall Street Is Already Betting on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Wall Street firms are placing institutional bets on prediction market platforms, signaling mainstream financial adoption of what was once a niche betting arena. This influx of serious money validates prediction markets as legitimate financial instruments and will likely drive massive liquidity increases that make these platforms more accurate and profitable for informed bettors.

articles.title: Traders Flip Senate Control Bet as Democrats Overtake Republicans on Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction market traders are now betting Democrats will control the Senate after the 2024 elections, reversing earlier odds that favored Republicans on both Kalshi and Polymarket. This flip signals either new polling data, campaign developments, or smart money moving in, giving bettors a real-time read on shifting electoral dynamics that polls might miss.

articles.title: Polymarket Five-Minute Bets Capture ‘Addictive’ Crypto Craze
articles.summary: Polymarket launched five-minute prediction bets that are apparently hooking crypto traders with their slot-machine-like rush of instant gratification. The move signals prediction markets are chasing the same dopamine-driven engagement that makes crypto trading and sports betting so profitable, which could either explode the user base or turn the whole space into a casino.

articles.title: AI agents are quietly rewriting prediction market trading
articles.summary: Autonomous AI agents are now placing bets and executing complex trading strategies across prediction markets without human intervention. This shift threatens to create massive information asymmetries where algorithmic traders with superior data processing capabilities systematically outperform human bettors.
